skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate and edit images through the inference.sh belt CLI using FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and related image models.
skill.summary: Run text-to-image, image editing, stitching, and upscaling workflows through inference.sh image models.
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command hits, network URLs, and one weak-cryptography blocker. Review found the weak-cryptography blocker is a false positive in descriptive text, while the command hits are mostly Markdown examples for the belt CLI. The skill still requires a Bash permission for belt and sends prompts or image URLs to external inference services, so it should publish with a clear warning.

medium_findings.title: Wildcard Bash Permission for External CLI
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash access for belt commands and provides multiple belt app run examples. This is expected for an inference.sh wrapper, but it enables external command execution and remote service calls when users follow the workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly grants Bash access to belt and documents commands that call external model apps. The use appears legitimate, but the capability is real and should be disclosed.

medium_findings.title: External Image Service and URL Inputs
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to use inference.sh services and includes image URL input examples. Prompts and referenced image URLs may be sent to third-party services, which creates privacy and data handling risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The networked service and URL inputs are directly documented in the skill. There is no evidence of secret exfiltration, but remote processing is central to the workflow.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Matches
low_findings.description: Most static external command matches are Markdown code fences, inline app identifiers, or example commands. They are not Ruby shell backticks and do not execute by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown content and CLI examples in SKILL.md, not executable Ruby code. They become active only if an agent or user chooses to run the documented commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hit Is Descriptive Text
low_findings.description: The high static blocker on line 3 appears to be a keyword match inside the skill description. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing implementation, or security-sensitive crypto use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML metadata describing image generation models and trigger phrases. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code or weak algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Examples
low_findings.description: The URL findings point to inference.sh documentation, an image preview, installation instructions, and placeholder image URL examples. These are expected for a CLI documentation skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or user-supplied image placeholders. I did not find evidence that the skill silently sends local data or credentials to those URLs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tooling Around Networked Model Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed Bash command scope and belt app run examples combine local command execution with external inference APIs. This pattern is not malicious here, but it requires user awareness.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and necessary for the skill's purpose. The reviewed file does not show obfuscation, credential theft, or prompt-injection instructions.

content.value_statement: Creative teams need fast access to many image models without switching tools. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through inference.sh belt commands for generation, editing, stitching, and upscaling.
actual_capabilities: Lists supported inference.sh image model app IDs and their intended uses.
Provides belt CLI commands for text-to-image generation.
Shows examples for image upscaling and stitching workflows.
Includes model-specific prompts for FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, and Reve.
Links to inference.sh documentation for running apps and image generation.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI and a working account.
Does not include local image generation models or local inference code.
Generated image quality depends on the selected remote model.
Users must manage privacy for prompts and image URLs sent to external services.

use_cases.title: Create Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate product images, social media graphics, and campaign concepts from short creative prompts.

use_cases.title: Prototype Game and Film Concepts
use_cases.description: Explore character, environment, and mood concepts across several image models from one CLI workflow.

use_cases.title: Enhance Existing Assets
use_cases.description: Upscale images or stitch multiple hosted images into a combined output through documented belt commands.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-image-generation skill to create a square image of a modern desk setup with soft natural light.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Model Choices
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose the best model for a visual style
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-image-generation skill to recommend a model for a cinematic poster with readable title text, then generate the image.

prompt_templates.title: Upscale a Hosted Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve an existing image from a URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-image-generation skill to upscale this hosted image for a presentation: [image URL].

prompt_templates.title: Run a Multi-Step Creative Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-image-generation skill to generate three product concept directions, choose the strongest one, and prepare an upscaling command for final output.

output_examples.input: Generate a product photo of a ceramic mug on a white studio background.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects a suitable image model, prepares a belt command with the prompt, and explains where the generated image result will appear.

output_examples.input: Create a cyberpunk city image in a wide format.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses a model that supports aspect ratio control and prepares a wide-format generation request.

output_examples.input: Upscale this image for a client presentation.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares an upscaling workflow using the provided image URL and reminds the user not to submit private assets.

best_practices: Review prompts and image URLs before sending them to external inference services.
Choose the model based on quality, speed, aspect ratio, and text rendering needs.
Start with concise prompts, then iterate with specific composition and lighting details.
anti_patterns: Do not submit confidential images, unreleased products, or private user data without approval.
Do not run belt commands before confirming the target model and input values.
Do not assume every listed model supports the same image size, aspect ratio, or editing mode.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the inference.sh belt CLI to run remote image model apps.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What must be installed first?
faq.answer: Users need the inference.sh belt CLI and access to the relevant image apps.

faq.question: Can it edit or upscale existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes examples for image editing, stitching, and Topaz upscaling workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe for private images?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and image URLs may be processed by external services.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses external CLI commands and networked image services, even though no malicious intent was found.
